pattern: Because the alphabet has only twenty-six letters, you can brute-force which single letter loses one occurrence and check if the remaining nonzero counts all match.
hints: After removing one letter, every letter that still appears must share the same count.
There are only twenty-six distinct letters, so trying to drop one occurrence of each is cheap.
For each present letter, decrement its count by one, drop it if it hits zero, and test whether all surviving counts are equal.

edge_inputs.description: two identical letters remove to one
one letter appears once extra over many equal letters
one unique surplus letter among equals
all equal already so removing breaks balance unless one letter

stress.description: random short words over a small alphabet to hit equalizable and non-equalizable cases
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, rng.randint(2, max(2, size)))
    k = rng.randint(1, 5)
    letters = 'abcde'[:k]
    return [''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: small alphabet makes balanced and near-balanced frequencies common
